---
description: "Use when writing, editing, or reviewing Prize Raffle JS modules. Covers module export pattern, random numbers, XSS, localStorage, naming, and comment language."
applyTo: "js/**"
---

# JS Module Conventions

## Module export — required pattern
```js
window.ModuleName = {
    publicFn,
    get prop() { return _private; },
    set prop(v) { _private = v; }
};
```
- `ModuleName` is PascalCase, matches filename (`data-manager.js` → `DataManager`)
- No `export` keyword, no ES modules

## Random numbers
- **Only** `window.RaffleEngine.secureRandom()` — never `Math.random()` or direct `crypto.getRandomValues()`
- `secureRandom()` is defined once in `raffle-engine.js`; all other modules call it via `window.RaffleEngine`

## XSS — innerHTML safety
- All user-supplied data inserted into `innerHTML` must go through `escapeHtml()` from `ui-controller.js`
- Numeric indices generated by code (not user input) in `onclick` are safe without escaping

## localStorage
```js
try {
    localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEYS.FOO, value);
} catch (e) {
    // handle gracefully
}
```
- Always wrap in `try-catch`
- Always use `STORAGE_KEYS` constants — never raw string keys

## Naming
| Kind | Style | Example |
|------|-------|---------|
| Functions / variables | `camelCase` | `addParticipant` |
| Constants | `UPPER_CASE` | `STORAGE_KEYS` |
| Module (window export) | `PascalCase` | `RaffleEngine` |

## Code style
- 4-space indent, max 120 chars per line
- `const` by default; `let` only when reassignment is needed; no `var`
- Arrow functions for callbacks; template literals for interpolation
- **All comments in Ukrainian**
